analysis_result: Log-rank test p-value = 0.2082 (test statistic = 1.5842). Comparison: G3 (PremalignantNF_G3, n=29, 9 events) vs benign NF subgroup (only Benign_NF-G4 present; n=20, 1 event). Note: Fig 1b source data contains NO cutaneous NF samples (Cutaneous_NF_G6 and Cutaneous_NF_G7 are absent from the sheet), so the benign comparator reduces to G4 alone. Based on these data the log-rank test is NOT statistically significant at p<0.05, which does not reproduce the paper's claim of significantly worse PFS in G3 compared with benign NF subgroups when using the 75-sample Fig 1b cohort.
denominator_used: N = 49 samples total for the two-group log-rank comparison: G3 = PremalignantNF_G3 (n=29, 9 recurrences); Benign NF = Benign_NF-G4 (n=20, 1 recurrence). Cutaneous_NF_G6 and Cutaneous_NF_G7 are not present in the Fig 1b sheet. Remaining 26 samples (MPNST_G1=8, MPNST_G2=8, Sporadic Spinal NF_G5=10) excluded from this two-group comparison.
assumptions_made: Used 'Time to Recurrence or Last Follow Up' (years) as duration; used 'Recurrence' (0/1) as event indicator; interpreted recurrence-free interval as progression-free survival (PFS); performed two-sided log-rank test via lifelines.statistics.logrank_test with default (no weighting) method; benign NF subgroup operationalized as union of G4+G6+G7, but since G6 and G7 are absent from Fig 1b, the benign comparator contained only G4 cases; no covariate adjustment; no stratification.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - File: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM10_ESM.xlsx, sheet 'Fig 1b' (75 rows x 3 cols).
   - Columns used: Methylation_Group, Recurrence (0/1), Time to Recurrence or Last Follow Up (years).
   - Methylation_Group distribution: PremalignantNF_G3=29, Benign_NF-G4=20, Sporadic Spinal NF_G5=10, MPNST_G1=8, MPNST_G2=8. No samples labeled Cutaneous_NF_G6 or Cutaneous_NF_G7 are present.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Start: 75 samples.
   - Filter: keep samples in G3 or in {G4, G6, G7}. G6 and G7 yield 0 rows -> benign set = G4 only.
   - G3 group: PremalignantNF_G3, n=29, 9 events (Recurrence=1).
   - Benign NF group: Benign_NF-G4, n=20, 1 event.
   - Final analytic cohort for log-rank: n = 49 (29 + 20).

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Duration: 'Time to Recurrence or Last Follow Up' (years).
   - Event: 'Recurrence' (1 = recurrence event; 0 = censored at last follow-up).
   - Group assignment: binary factor {G3, benign_NF} derived from Methylation_Group column as described above.

D. COMPUTATION
   - Two-group log-rank test via lifelines.statistics.logrank_test with arrays of durations and event indicators per group; default parameters (no weighting).
   - Also fit Kaplan-Meier curves per group for descriptive summary.

E. RESULT
   - Log-rank test statistic = 1.5842; p-value = 0.2082 (p = 2.08e-01); -log2(p) = 2.264.
   - Median PFS: G3 = 11.79 years; Benign NF (G4 only) = not reached (only 1/20 events).
   - Conclusion: p = 0.21, not statistically significant at the 0.05 threshold.

F. CAVEATS
   - Fig 1b source data does NOT contain Cutaneous_NF_G6 or Cutaneous_NF_G7, so the 'benign NF subgroups' comparator was restricted to G4 only. The paper's claim of significantly worse PFS in G3 vs benign NF subgroups may rely on a different cohort/figure (e.g., Fig 1f/g) that includes cutaneous NFs.
   - Few events in the benign arm (1/20) limit statistical power.
   - Interpretation assumes standard log-rank test (non-weighted); the paper may report a different test or comparison (e.g., G3 vs all non-MPNST benign including G5; G3 vs each benign subgroup separately).
   - No adjustment for covariates; no competing-risk analysis.
   - With only benign_NF-G4 available as the benign comparator from Fig 1b, this analysis does not reproduce a significant p-value. Using a broader or different benign composite (with additional cutaneous NF data potentially located in another supplementary sheet) could yield a significantly worse PFS result for G3.
